title: silene corrugata var. mogadorensis
intro: Silene corrugata var. mogadorensis, also known as Mogadore catchfly, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Silene corrugata plant, a flowering plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The medicinal properties of Silene corrugata var. mogadorensis have been attributed to its ability to promote tissue repair, reduce swelling, and prevent infection, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, Silene corrugata var. mogadorensis is often used as a tincture or infused in oil to create topical creams and salves for skin care and wound healing.
